action.reasoning: After 1M steps of walk training (tiny net), success_rate still 0. Walk mean reward only 4.9 — far below 700 bar. Analysis: (1) CPU training with single env (DummyVecEnv) is extremely slow (1831s for 100k steps). With 8.77h left, we must speed up rollouts using parallel SubprocVecEnv to get millions of steps per task. (2) We need to test a scalable training loop: SubprocVecEnv with spawn, 8-16 workers, measure time to 1M steps. (3) We must decide which tasks to prioritize; reach (12000 bar) likely impossible with this compute, so we'll skip it or train it last. Focus on walk, stand, run, sit_simple, pole, door, push, package — those with bars ≤1500 and plausible. (4) Keep network moderate (256,256) and try 2M steps for walk first to see if reward approaches 700, then expand to multiple tasks. Decision: Write train_multi.py using SubprocVecEnv, train walk for 2M steps (16 envs), measure wall time. Use spawn start method. Run it. After training, submit eval to gauge walk performance improvement.

command_output.stdout: [h1hand-walk-v0] Starting training with 16 envs, 2000000 steps, arch [256, 256, 256]
